behaviors.description: Clause extractor identifies all key term types from contracts
behaviors.justification: Extraction quality depends on real LLM capability and prompt design. Mocking the LLM would hide whether the actual model can reliably extract all required clause types (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) from diverse contracts. Different models or prompt formulations may extract different completeness. Only real LLM execution reveals extraction gaps.
behaviors.test_description: Run real clause extractor on diverse contracts including edge cases; verify all required key term types (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) are extracted; run repeatedly on similar contracts to verify consistency; test contracts with missing/ambiguous terms to verify robustness

behaviors.description: Risk assessor flags problematic language including indirect/euphemistic phrasing
behaviors.justification: Detecting subtle, indirect risk language is fundamentally an LLM judgment task. Mocking the assessor would always return 'risk detected' without revealing whether the real model can catch indirect or euphemistic phrasing. The configured risk policy provides parameters, but the detection capability itself requires real LLM execution. Different models will have different sensitivity to subtle language.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real risk assessor on contracts with known subtle and explicit risks; verify both direct problematic clauses and indirect/euphemistic equivalents are flagged; test borderline language cases; include real-world problematic clause variations to ensure the policy is actually applied by the model

behaviors.description: Compliance checker determines contract meets jurisdiction regulatory requirements
behaviors.justification: Regulatory compliance determination requires the LLM to understand jurisdiction-specific requirements and match contract terms against them. Mocking would hide whether the real model actually possesses and applies jurisdiction-specific regulatory knowledge. If the system queries an external regulatory API, minimum level may be System. But assuming LLM-based compliance with configured jurisdiction rules, real LLM execution is needed to verify regulatory understanding.
behaviors.test_description: Run real compliance checker on contracts known to be compliant and non-compliant across multiple jurisdictions; verify correct pass/fail determinations for each jurisdiction; test edge cases near regulatory boundaries; verify the checker correctly rejects contracts missing required jurisdiction-specific elements

behaviors.description: Merge step preserves conflicting assessments with attribution to source reviewer
behaviors.justification: The merge function is pure logic that detects conflicts and preserves both assessments with attribution. No LLM or I/O is involved in the merge step itself. We can construct realistic reviewer output objects in-process (e.g., Pydantic models with the right structure) and test conflict detection and attribution without needing real reviewers. Failure modes are merge logic bugs, not LLM capability gaps.
behaviors.test_description: Create test cases with structured reviewer outputs including cases where reviewers assess the same clause differently (e.g., extractor labels as 'standard' while risk assessor marks 'high risk'); verify merge preserves all three assessments; verify each assessment is clearly attributed to its source reviewer; test multiple conflicts in same document

behaviors.description: Full pipeline dispatches reviewers, collects results, and merges without losing findings
behaviors.justification: This tests the full agentic pipeline: dispatching to three independent real reviewers in parallel, collecting their results, and merging. 'Real contract' and 'without losing findings' indicate we must verify end-to-end orchestration with actual LLM calls. Failures could occur in: parallel dispatch, result collection timeout/error handling, data loss during merge, or reviewer failures. These are only caught by running the full workflow with real reviewers and real contract documents.
behaviors.test_description: Execute complete pipeline on diverse real contracts of varying complexity; verify all three reviewer results are collected and present in output; verify merged report contains all findings from all reviewers (no data loss); run on contracts designed to produce reviewer disagreements; test timeout and error handling scenarios
